title: Putting our heads together: interpersonal neural synchronization as a biological mechanism for shared intentionality.

assets.abstract: Shared intentionality, or collaborative interactions in which individuals have a shared goal and must coordinate their efforts, is a core component of human interaction. However, the biological bases of shared intentionality and, specifically, the processes by which the brain adjusts to the sharing of common goals, remain largely unknown. Using functional near infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS), coordination of cerebral hemodynamic activation was found in subject pairs when completing a puzzle togeth

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Social Incarnation — intersubjectivity, embodied empathy, social predictive processing.
